organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The College of Management - Shaping the Future of Higher Education in Israel

For over 45 years, the College of Management has been at the forefront of higher education in Israel. As the first private academic institution recognized by the Council for Higher Education, we continue to lead in innovation and adaptation to the evolving needs of the job market.

9,000   students | 85,000 alumni | Diverse Bachelor's and Master's programs

Innovation at our core: Integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into teaching, learning, and research

Connected to industry: Curricula tailored to current market demands

Leading fields of study: Business Administration, Law, Computer Science, Economics, Communications, and more

Advanced campus in Rishon LeZion, central Israel

At the College of Management, we are preparing Israel's future leaders. Join us and be at the forefront of academic innovation and excellence!

#HigherEducation #AcademicInnovation #ProfessionalTraining #ArtificialIntelligence #FutureOfWork

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Currently, the youth village consists of a high-school and a boarding school. The school hosts the children of the local communities and offers technological and comprehensive studies. Its present principal is Nurit Cohen.

The school was awarded by the Ministry of Education best absorption of new education workers in 2015-16. In addition, it received the Education Award for cultural collaboration from the Teachers'​ Association.

The boarding school admits students from all around Israel as well as students from abroad. It is considered to be one of the leading boarding schools in Israel and recently has received the Best Boarding School Education Award.

Mosenson high school offers a variety of unique classes, including sports – such as dancing, swimming, basketball, football, and personal sports. It also offers classes of biomedicine, electronics, film, Nachshon Program, and general studies.

Students of the Naale program, from North America, Europe, South Africa, and other countries study at the youth village, where there is also a special program for students who do not speak Hebrew, to enable them to become part of the Israeli society.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Mosenson Elite Academy

companyResolutionResult.description:
Aman group is an international company and one of the top 5  leading IT solutions supplier in Israel, with a wide portfolio of services and products  in the fields of data integration, virtualization and networking, Information & cyber security, command and control, software development, knowledge management, UX&design, Output management verification and more.

Aman Group  leverages technology and innovation to solve diverse IT challenges and power Israel’s largest companies.

Aman Group represents a wide range of advanced solutions, each a leader in its own field, most of them exclusively represented. 
Among them:
Informatica, Palo Alto Networks, Citrix, Accellion, Proofpoint, Rubrik, SAP, DocuSign, XPLG and more. 

Based on our successful activity and partnership, Aman was chosen by its partners to become a distributor in East Europe. 

Aman Group also provides a wide range of outsourcing services in several models – some of them are unique in the Israeli market - from overall responsibility over organizational IT systems, Data Centers, operation and maintenance of organizational communication networks, comprehensive Help Desk services, systems' infrastructure management, management of data protection and communications infrastructures, to Near-shore and off -shore solutions and providing experts in specific fields according to the organization's targeted needs.

Among our customers are most of the leading Israeli companies in the industry banking and finances, health, telecom, Security &defense, government /public sectors, Hi-tech and more.
companyResolutionResult.name: Aman Group

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

Outpace everyone with the best AI work platform. 

monday.com brings people, workflows, and AI agents together on one platform, so teams can plan, execute, and deliver with exponential impact.
monday sidekick is the digital worker that thinks with you, creates for you, and moves work forward. monday vibe lets you build custom business software in minutes. AI Blocks and AI Workflows automate everything at scale. And monday agents multiply your team's capacity: an always-on AI workforce that handles specialized tasks across every department.
From work management to CRM, dev, service, and campaigns, it all runs on one connected, context-aware platform.
Trusted by 250,000+ customers, including over 60% of the Fortune 500, across 200+ countries.

monday.com has offices in Tel Aviv, New York, Miami, Chicago, Denver, London, Warsaw, Sydney, Melbourne, São Paulo, and Tokyo.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Basmach - IDF School of Computer Science

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Hebrew University of Jerusalem is Israel's first university, formed in 1918 and opened in 1925. Today, there are over 23,000 students enrolled at the University, including undergraduates, master's degree students, and doctoral candidates.

The university has four campuses: Mount Scopus, The Edmond J. Safra (Givat Ram) Campus, and Ein Kerem (in Jerusalem), and Rehovot.

Hebrew University Alumni are invited to visit https://alumni.huji.ac.il

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Reichman University (previously IDC Herzliya), the first private institution of higher education in Israel, opened its doors in 1994 thanks to the vision of Prof. Uriel Reichman. Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies. 

Our basic outlook, which is rooted in the twin concepts of individual freedom and responsibility, emphasizes student entrepreneurship and leadership alongside a commitment to community service.

Follow us on all social media channels >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

מטרת אוניברסיטת רייכמן (לשעבר הבינתחומי) היא להכשיר את מנהיגות העתיד של מדינת ישראל, באמצעות השכלה בינתחומית, המשלבת ידע תיאורטי מעמיק עם הכשרה מעשית, ובהנחיית סגל מרצים וחוקרים מהאוניברסיטאות המובילות בעולם ובישראל. גישתנו היא הקפדה על מצוינות במחקר ובהוראה, לצד דגש על אחריות חברתית ומעורבות בקהילה. מאז הקמתו, הצליח הבינתחומי הרצליה להיות חלוץ בתחומים רבים ולהוביל מהפכה במערכת ההשכלה הגבוהה בישראל, שאת עקבותיה ניתן לראות כיום במוסדות אקדמיים אחרים.

עקבו אחרינו בכל הרשתות החברתיות >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Ashkelon Academic College (AAC) staff is pleased to invite you to its spacious campus in the Ashkelon Education Center and offers you the chance to integrate into an academic college track. The college provides a broad range of academic options in a friendly, supportive atmosphere with varied cultural and social activities.

For thousands of young adults, AAC is a leading institution. Currently considered one of the largest and most prestigious academic centers in Israel, its main areas of study are the social sciences, health sciences, exact sciences and humanities for Bachelor and Masters degrees. AAC has an experienced teaching team, including some of academia’s top professors and senior lecturers.

The college’s administrative and academic staff works to assist students throughout their years of study, through special programs and workshops geared to offer college graduates tools which enable smooth transitioning into the workplace, provision of scholarships, or personal mentoring to completion of studies. Students enjoy an advanced learning environment which includes labs, a well-stocked library, direct access to essential databases, rooms for shared studying, dorms, a students’ club, and a gym.

companyResolutionResult.description:
NESS is one of Israel’s leading companies in the field of information systems, having reached this status thanks to our employees. As our main asset, for more than 25 years our employees have been and continue to be responsible for the cementing of our core business and the values on which the loyalty of our customer relationships is based.
As a company with 60 years of accumulated experience in responsible and successful management of some of the largest and most complex projects undertaken in Israel, we implement practically what it means to be the leading BSM company in Israel: utmost professionalism, implementation of quality processes and complex methodologies, risk management, training of skilled manpower who possess both business savvy and technological knowledge, and punctual project delivery to customer's absolute satisfaction.

Discover our career opportunities here: www.ness-tech.co.il/joinus
or send your CV directly to linkedin@ness-tech.co.il

Follow us on Instagram: @ness_israel | TikTok: @Life.at.ness
Find us on Facebook: @nesstechnologiesisrael

For more details regarding the use of your personal data, your rights in relation thereto, and information concerning the retention of such data and its transfer to third parties, please refer to our Privacy Policy as published on the Company’s website.

companyResolutionResult.description:
ShopperAI brings real-time awareness to retail, so the floor flows, the shelves stay full, and shoppers stay loyal.

Using the cameras already installed in your stores, we turn everyday footage into actionable intelligence. That means teams know exactly where to go, what to fix, and when to act, before shoppers notice something’s missing.

We see the retail floor not as a logistics system, but as a human environment. Every empty shelf is a missed promise.

Every blocked aisle is a moment of unnecessary frustration. ShopperAI helps retailers prevent those moments, and turn everyday execution into a competitive edge.

Led by a multidisciplinary team of hands-on retail operators, AI experts, and product builders, we’re building the tools that empower people, not replace them.

Managers gain clarity. Staff gain control. Shoppers feel cared for, and keep coming back.

companyResolutionResult.description: Nagomi Security gives enterprise security teams the control to eliminate exposure, faster and at scale. As the execution layer of Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM), Nagomi unifies asset visibility, contextual prioritization, remediation guidance, and performance reporting in a single platform. At its core is Exposure Lens, the only engine that correlates assets, controls, vulnerabilities, and threats to show risk in context across subsidiaries and business units. By validating defenses and directing fixes to the right owners, Nagomi ensures issues are resolved instead of tracked, closing exposures faster, strengthening defenses continuously, and delivering measurable progress for both security and business leaders. Recognized by Gartner® as a Cool Vendor, Nagomi is a pioneer in Automated Security Control Assessment (ASCA), helping organizations operationalize exposure management and drive down risk with the tools they already own.

For over 45 years, the College of Management has been at the forefront of higher education in Israel. As the first private academic institution recognized by the Council for Higher Education, we continue to lead in innovation and adaptation to the evolving needs of the job market.

9,000   students | 85,000 alumni | Diverse Bachelor's and Master's programs

Innovation at our core: Integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into teaching, learning, and research

Connected to industry: Curricula tailored to current market demands

Leading fields of study: Business Administration, Law, Computer Science, Economics, Communications, and more

Advanced campus in Rishon LeZion, central Israel

At the College of Management, we are preparing Israel's future leaders. Join us and be at the forefront of academic innovation and excellence!

#HigherEducation #AcademicInnovation #ProfessionalTraining #ArtificialIntelligence #FutureOfWork

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ono Academic College was established in 1995 as a private, non-profit academic institution and has set itself two principal goals:

A. Developing and studying applied academic programs, which will enable graduates to integrate directly into the labor market, while maintaining the highest level of academic standards and teaching quality.

B. Giving top priority to the social problems of the State of Israel, by finding solutions for various sectors of the population, providing the opportunity for higher academic studies and, as a result, the ability to play an active role in the Israeli workforce.

Within this framework, we have developed study courses for outstanding students, enabling them to fulfill their potential and devote their time to multidisciplinary studies in more than one faculty, according to their choice.
In addition, Ono is proud to be the only higher education institute in Israel to include four academic hours of compulsory community work. We regard this personal commitment as an educational message of the highest level, which will result in the students' appreciation of Ono Academic College's worldview. Developing social sensitivity and awareness of the needs of one's community is an integral part of training future lawyers, accountants, and business professionals.

Ono Academic College is home to over 23,000 active students currently enrolled in various programs, including the undergraduate program in Law (LLB) and the undergraduate and graduate programs in Business Administration (BA and MBA) with a variety of specializations: Marketing and Advertising, Finance, Information Systems Analysis, Accounting, and Human Resources Management.

To date, over 60,000 graduates have successfully completed their studies at Ono Academic College and have gone on to make a significant impact in their respective fields.

We wish you success in your application for acceptance and hope to see you among us in the coming academic year.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ono Academic College

companyResolutionResult.description:
At Alta, we use data and AI to drive revenue growth at every level of your business. Our AI Revenue Workforce agents empower everyone on the revenue team to contribute to growth—from strategic leadership decisions to flawless execution.

For sales and marketing teams, Alex and Katie, our AI SDR agents, handle the execution. Alex, our AI Calling Agent, manages outbound outreach, inbound call qualification, follow-ups, and even account management—driving more meetings, faster responses, and new revenue opportunities at scale. Katie identifies and researches the most relevant prospects, sends hyper-personalized multi-channel campaigns via email and LinkedIn, and schedules meetings for your sales team—helping you scale efficiently and grow faster.

Meet Luna, our AI RevOps agent, designed to support leadership with strategic insights. Luna automates data analysis, generates real-time reports, uncovers actionable business insights, and notifies you of any crucial metric changes or anomalies—all without manual effort.

With Alta, your entire revenue team is connected and aligned. No more disconnect between strategy and execution—our AI agents ensure everything is streamlined. By saving time on manual work, Alta empowers everyone in your company with data insights and AI automation to drive growth seamlessly.
companyResolutionResult.name: Alta | AI GTM System of Actions

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Welcome to she codes;!
she codes; is a community of women in technology, created to reach 50% of software developers woman within a decade in Israel. Founded in 2013 by Ruth Polachek, today she codes; includes approximately 50,000 members.
Our members include women who want to learn software development or make a career shift into high-tech, developers who want to advance their skills and network with other women in high-tech and high school students who are learning to program. Our core values are Believe in Yourself, Perseverance, and Community.
We operate over 40 branches all over Israel. Branches include programming learning tracks and project co-working with support from community volunteers. In addition, there are enriching and interesting lectures at the branches which grant tools and vital programming knowledge, as well as coding skills, inspirational and career lectures. These lectures are also open to women who are not members of the branch.
Volunteer opportunities at she codes; contribute to the personal and professional development of members.
Branches, learning track planning, events, and more are all volunteer-run.

A new learning cycle opens at the beginning of every quarter in each of the branches around the country.
The meetings and the activity in the branches form a framework which allows personal and professional development.
Even more important, the meetings give an opportunity to meet more women - to form professional relationships with experienced women who love to program and talk tech.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: she codes;

companyResolutionResult.description:
Reichman University (previously IDC Herzliya), the first private institution of higher education in Israel, opened its doors in 1994 thanks to the vision of Prof. Uriel Reichman. Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies. 

Our basic outlook, which is rooted in the twin concepts of individual freedom and responsibility, emphasizes student entrepreneurship and leadership alongside a commitment to community service.

Follow us on all social media channels >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Tel Aviv-Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

resumePosition.details:
• Built and ran the full go-to-market solo: outbound prospecting (calls, cold email/messages), discovery, proposals, negotiation, delivery, and ongoing client management for SMB customers in Israel.
• Closed $1,500+ projects by targeting businesses with weak web presence, running needs-based discovery, and converting outreach into booked meetings and signed work through direct follow-up and clear value framing.

• Generated $317K+ in closed revenue and $19K+ in monthly expansion revenue by identifying and capturing growth opportunities across a 2,500-account book.
• Executed a hybrid prospecting strategy targeting both new logo acquisition and service expansion through high-volume outreach via calls, emails, and messages.
• Led needs-based discovery sessions to qualify prospects, identifying key business pain points and expansion triggers to drive increased account ACV.
• Managed high-velocity sales cycles for 1,000+ accounts, overseeing the process from initial qualification and pricing quotes to negotiation and closing.
• Architected the company's BDR sales playbooks, standardizing outbound sequences, discovery frameworks, and objection-handling scripts to improve team ramp time.
• Multi-threaded across SMB & Mid Market accounts to build consensus with key decision-makers, including CEOs, Finance leads, and Marketing Directors.
• Tiered accounts to prioritize high-intent prospects, resulting in a consistent 70%+ conversion rate on opportunities.

• Owned the people-operations function for 170 personnel across multiple functions, serving as the escalation point for time-sensitive, high-stakes personnel issues.
• Led 7 department heads and drove structured onboarding and process discipline to keep teams aligned under daily, weekly, and monthly deadlines.
• Managed $25K annual budget and delivered recurring 3-month cross-unit initiatives by assigning owners, tracking milestones, and unblocking execution for senior stakeholders.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The College of Management - Shaping the Future of Higher Education in Israel

For over 45 years, the College of Management has been at the forefront of higher education in Israel. As the first private academic institution recognized by the Council for Higher Education, we continue to lead in innovation and adaptation to the evolving needs of the job market.

9,000   students | 85,000 alumni | Diverse Bachelor's and Master's programs

Innovation at our core: Integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into teaching, learning, and research

Connected to industry: Curricula tailored to current market demands

Leading fields of study: Business Administration, Law, Computer Science, Economics, Communications, and more

Advanced campus in Rishon LeZion, central Israel

At the College of Management, we are preparing Israel's future leaders. Join us and be at the forefront of academic innovation and excellence!

#HigherEducation #AcademicInnovation #ProfessionalTraining #ArtificialIntelligence #FutureOfWork

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Founded in 2020, CoLeague is a distinctive co-learning platform designed for CEOs, VPs, and organic management teams to EXPLORE, LEARN, and SHARE valuable insights and best practices that address the pressing leadership and management challenges of today's dynamic business landscape.

At CoLeague, our platform seamlessly blends curated content with advanced peer learning techniques. We prioritize creating a safe and supportive environment where participants can reflect on:
✔️ Their organization  
✔️ Their management teams  
✔️ and themselves  
by leveraging the perspectives and collective wisdom of the entire group.

CoLeague offers three distinct programs, each tailored to specific audiences:
🔷 CoLeague CEO groups – Exclusively for active CEOs  and GMs
This program consists of 10 monthly, face-to-face sessions, each lasting 3.5 hours.  
The group is limited to ~10 CEOs, ensuring focused discussions and peer interaction.  
Website: https://www.coleague.co.il/CEO

🔷 CoLeague C-Level groups – Created for active manamegement members (not more than one from a company).  
This program includes 10 monthly, face-to-face sessions of 3.5 hours each.  
Each group is limited to ~10 participants, fostering meaningful collaboration and learning.  
Website: https://www.coleague.co.il/c-level

🔷 CoLeague IN – Designed specifically for organic management teams  
The structure and content of this program are uniquely customized to fit the needs of each management team.  
Website: https://www.coleague.co.il/management

Join us at CoLeague to elevate your leadership capabilities and thrive in the modern era of business, with dedicated programs tailored to your role!
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: CoLeague

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

companyResolutionResult.description:
The J6 & Cyber Defense Directorate in the Israel Defense Forces is responsible for cyber defense, communication, wireless transmission, computerization, and control over intelligence information in the IDF. 

The Cyber Defense Directorate provides commanders in the field with the appropriate technology needed to manage a situation in the best possible way. The Directorate's main objectives are to initiate, develop, and strengthen the IDF's technological systems. With growing technology, the Directorate has become a center and leader in combating cyber threats towards Israel.
companyResolutionResult.name: J6 & Cyber Defense Directorate, IDF

resumePosition.details:
• Developed C++ models simulating CPU and SoC microarchitecture, including pipeline behavior, data path and memory systems.
• Designed performance features to explore microarchitectural optimizations.
• Workloads analysis and performance tuning using industry-standard benchmarks including SPEC, GKB, HiNext, and LM.

• Developed C/C++ models for modem and processing.
• Correlated CA & functional behavior with hardware models.
• Collaborated with architecture and system teams to enhance model reliability.

• Developed embedded C/C++ software includes FW and logic optimization.
• Integrated and debugged custom microwave networking hardware.

• Built test frameworks for DLP products.
• Performed QA and integration testing on Windows platforms.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Bar-Ilan is the fastest-growing university in the past seven years.

The university is at the forefront of research in fields such as energy and sustainability, medical and life sciences, cyber, nanotechnology, natural language processing, and more. 

Alongside academic excellence, the university places great emphasis on community involvement, through clinics open to the general public, and on the well-being of its students.
